steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence introduces "certain grains" as the broad driver of transformation. The second sentence then names the specific grains — maize and wheat. The blank must bridge that move from the general category to the concrete examples within it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'by comparison,': signals that two things are being contrasted against each other. No second item is being compared here — maize and wheat are examples of the grains already mentioned, not a contrasting case. ✗
  • Choice B — 'second of all,': implies a numbered sequence that started with "first of all." No such first point appeared in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — 'alternatively,': signals a different option being offered in place of something. Nothing is being replaced or offered as a substitute here. ✗
  • Choice D — 'in particular,': zooms into a specific subset of a broader category. It correctly narrows from "certain grains" to the specific grains — maize and wheat — that drove the transformation. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
